title: Projected Lineup: Ducks Look to Bounce Back Against Red Wings on April 3
body:
The St. Louis Blues' recent string of struggles has left their head coach, Jim Montgomery, with little choice but to shake things up. Following a disheartening 2-1 overtime loss to the Los Angeles Kings on Wednesday, Montgomery hinted at making significant changes to his lineup, a promise he seems poised to keep.

In the aftermath of their latest defeat, Montgomery emphasized the need for his team to produce more than just sporadic flashes of brilliance. With the team still reeling from a subpar performance, the coach's words now hang in the balance, as he and his players look to rebound in their next outing. When asked about the possibility of changes to the lineup, Montgomery's response was straightforward: "there are going to be changes." And judging by his tone, those changes may not be limited to minor adjustments.

The Blues' loss to the Kings marked their second consecutive defeat, dealing a significant blow to their playoff aspirations. With the team currently trailing in the league standings, time is of the essence. Montgomery's roster, which boasts an impressive collection of talent, has failed to gel on multiple occasions this season, resulting in a frustrating lack of consistency. As a result, the pressure is mounting on the coach to find a solution, and he appears willing to take bold action to shake things up.

The decision to make changes to the lineup will undoubtedly be a delicate balancing act, as Montgomery seeks to strike a chord between addressing the team's woes while also avoiding a collapse in morale. Given the team's wealth of talent, the potential for growth is immense, but it will require Montgomery to pinpoint the exact areas where adjustments are needed. A tweak here or there may be insufficient to spark a turnaround, forcing the coach to consider more drastic measures.

Montgomery's willingness to make changes also highlights the growing sense of urgency within the organization. With the playoffs on the horizon, the team's failure to gain traction threatens to derail their postseason aspirations. The stakes are high, and the coach's actions will be scrutinized intensely in the coming days. Will Montgomery opt for a full-scale overhaul or a more subtle approach? Whatever the decision, one thing is certain: the St. Louis Blues will look different when they take the ice next.

In the end, the onus is on Montgomery and his team to deliver a performance worthy of the talent at their disposal. With his words now hanging in the balance, the coach will need to walk the fine line between making the necessary adjustments and preserving the team's fragile confidence. As they prepare to face the Anaheim Ducks on April 3, the Blues will be eager to prove that they still have what it takes to be a legitimate playoff contender.
